data.title: OpenClaw widens reach amid CVE-2026-25253 exploit chain
data.description:
OpenClaw ClawHub malicious skills, Atomic Stealer, CVE-2026-25253 hit SSH, wallet and API keys via Markdown lures and RCE chain; rotate keys and patch.
source: https://coincu.com/news/openclaw-widens-reach-amid-cve-2026-25253-exploit-chain/

data.title: Japan's Manufacturing Activity Remains Strong for Third Month
data.description: Japan's manufacturing sector continued to expand for the third consecutive month, driven by robust production levels. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this sustained growth reflects a positive corporate outlook as Prime Minister Takaichi gears up to advance her investment strategies. The consistent expansion in manufacturing suggests a stable economic environment, supporting the government's economic initiatives.

data.title: Norway Denies Joining U.S.-Led Peace Committee
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump recently stated that Norway had agreed to join a 'Peace Committee.' According to Jin10, this claim was refuted by Norwegian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Arne Jorem on February 19. Jorem reiterated that Norway will not participate in the U.S.-initiated 'Peace Committee.'

data.title: White House Advocates Limited Stablecoin Rewards
data.description: Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X that the White House is advocating for limited stablecoin rewards. The administration is urging banks to advance crypto legislation, emphasizing the need for regulatory clarity in the digital currency sector. This move is part of a broader effort to integrate stablecoins into the financial system while ensuring consumer protection and financial stability.

data.title: ZeruAI Launches AgentScan on Base Chain
data.description: On February 20, ZeruAI announced the launch of its artificial intelligence agent discovery page, AgentScan, on the Base chain. According to BlockBeats, agents can register, generate behavior-based zScores, and be publicly discovered. AgentScan is built on ERC-8004 and utilizes ZeruAI's behavioral intelligence technology, integrating data from various registration institutions. Additionally, agents can install AgentScan skills via npm.

data.title: Standard Chartered CFO Departure and HSBC Acquisition Impact Earnings Reports
data.description: Standard Chartered's unexpected departure of its finance chief is set to have significant implications as the bank prepares to report its earnings. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential impact of this leadership change on the bank's financial performance. Meanwhile, HSBC's recent multibillion-dollar acquisition of a bank stake is also under scrutiny as it prepares to release its earnings report. The financial community is closely watching how these developments will influence the banks' strategies and market positions. Both institutions are navigating complex financial landscapes, and their upcoming earnings reports are expected to provide insights into their future directions.

data.title: FreeRide AI Launches Live Quantitative Trading Competition
data.description: FreeRide AI has announced a seven-day live quantitative trading competition commencing on February 24. According to NS3.AI, the event will feature eight prominent AI models from China and the United States, competing in real-time cryptocurrency markets. The competition focuses on autonomous trading with institutional-grade risk controls. Participants are invited to predict outcomes to earn rewards without the need to invest principal. Following the event, FreeRide plans to invite community users to test its AI Trading Agent, which integrates advanced AI capabilities with professional trading standards.

data.title: Japan's Fiscal Package Raises Concerns Over National Debt and Bitcoin Impact
data.description: Japan's government has unveiled a fiscal package that includes record spending, tax cuts, and debt-financed deficits, resulting in national debt surpassing 250% of GDP. According to NS3.AI, this development heightens pressure on the Bank of Japan to increase interest rates, a move that historically leads to substantial Bitcoin selloffs due to yen carry trade unwinds. Despite this, the ongoing growth in debt strengthens Bitcoin's long-term appeal as a safeguard against sovereign debt risks and currency depreciation.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Former Ukrainian Commander Allegedly Approved Nord Stream Pipeline Sabotage
data.description:
On February 20, Jin10 reported that Germany's Der Spiegel magazine cited sources claiming that Valeriy Zaluzhnyi, the former commander of the Ukrainian military and Ukraine's ambassador to the UK, approved the sabotage of the Nord Stream gas pipelines. According to Jin10, the report states that the decision was made by Zaluzhnyi without the approval of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, indicating that the Zelensky administration was unaware of the sabotage plan.

It is reported that the U.S. Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) was aware of the plan to explode the Nord Stream pipelines beforehand. In 2022, CIA personnel reportedly met with Ukrainian officials in Kyiv, where Ukrainian experts explained the idea of destroying the gas pipelines to the Americans.

data.title: Trump Faces Backlash from MAHA Moms Over Glyphosate Order
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is experiencing a significant backlash from a group known as the MAHA moms, following his recent order concerning glyphosate. New York Times posted on X that this decision has been described as the 'final straw' for many women who feel deceived by the administration's actions. The sentiment among these women is one of betrayal, as they believe the promises made to them have not been fulfilled. This development highlights growing discontent among a key demographic that previously supported Trump, potentially impacting his political standing.

data.title: Copper Prices Decline Amid Rising Inventories and Weak Demand
data.description: Copper is on track for its third consecutive weekly decline as inventories monitored by exchanges continue to rise due to reduced demand from industrial users. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing challenges faced by the copper market. The accumulation of stockpiles reflects a slowdown in industrial activity, which has been impacting the demand for the metal. This trend is contributing to the downward pressure on copper prices, as market participants remain cautious about the near-term outlook. The situation underscores the broader concerns about the global economic environment and its effect on commodity markets.

data.title: Bitcoin's Price Decline Not Linked to Quantum Computing Concerns, Says Developer
data.description:
Bitcoin's recent price drop is not attributed to fears surrounding quantum computing, according to Bitcoin developer Matt Carallo. According to Cointelegraph, Carallo expressed his disagreement with the notion that Bitcoin's current valuation is significantly impacted by quantum risks during an appearance on the Unchained podcast with journalist Laura Shin. He argued that if quantum computing were truly a concern, Ethereum would be outperforming Bitcoin. However, Ether (ETH) has seen a 58% decline since a major crypto market crash in early October, currently trading at $1,957.

Carallo's remarks come amid discussions among Bitcoin enthusiasts who suggest that apprehensions about quantum computing's impact on blockchain technology have contributed to Bitcoin's 46% drop from its October peak of $126,100 to its current trading value of $67,162, as reported by CoinMarketCap. Some Bitcoin users have criticized the blockchain's developers for not advancing quickly enough to make the network quantum-resistant, while the Ethereum Foundation has announced its commitment to long-term post-quantum readiness as part of its security strategy.

Carallo acknowledged the long-term risks posed by quantum computing to Bitcoin but emphasized that market makers do not perceive it as an immediate threat. He suggested that the Bitcoin community might be seeking a scapegoat for the cryptocurrency's lackluster performance. Carallo proposed that Bitcoin's price decline is more likely due to its competition for capital against emerging technologies like artificial intelligence, which he described as a "massive new investment class" that demands substantial capital.

Not all Bitcoin advocates share Carallo's perspective. Charles Edwards, founder of Capriole Investments, stated at Cointelegraph's LONGITUDE event on February 12 that the risk of quantum computing should be factored into Bitcoin's valuation until the network becomes quantum-resistant. Edwards emphasized the need to discount Bitcoin's value based on this risk until it is addressed.

Meanwhile, entrepreneur Kevin O’Leary expressed in December that utilizing quantum computing to compromise Bitcoin may not be the most efficient use of resources, suggesting that the technology holds greater potential in fields like medical research. In May 2025, BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager, updated its registration statement for the iShares Bitcoin ETF (IBIT) to caution investors about the potential risks quantum computing poses to the integrity of the Bitcoin network.

data.title: Web3 Asset Data Reveals Top Unfollowed Projects on X
data.description:
In the past week, token trading tool Pepe Boost has emerged as the most unfollowed project by top influencers on X, according to ChainCatcher. Influential figures such as Blue Fox (@lanhubiji), Momo (@momochenming), and Sleeping in the Rain (@0xSleepinRain) have recently unfollowed this project.

Other projects that have seen significant unfollowing by top personalities on X include Calamity, Anoma Network, and Huma Finance.

data.title: Goldman Sachs: Gold prices will slowly climb to $5,400 by the end of the year.
data.description: Goldman Sachs predicts that gold prices will gradually climb, reaching $5,400 per ounce by the end of 2026, with diversification providing upside potential. Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs expects central bank gold purchases to accelerate again in 2026, at the pace seen in 2025. Their core view is that central bank demand, coupled with increased holdings by private investors (primarily in response to Fed rate cuts), will support a steady rise in gold prices. Goldman Sachs notes that if private sector diversification efforts (especially call option structures) increase, the market will face significant upside risks. Furthermore, while the medium-term trend remains upward, significant volatility is possible. (Golden Ten)

data.title: Freeride AI is hosting a US-China AI model live trading team competition, with a prediction airdrop event launching on February 24th.
data.description: FreeRide, an AI-powered automated trading platform, announced today that it will hold a 7-day live quantitative trading competition, "AI Trading Battle Royale," on February 24th. The platform grants its AI a high degree of autonomy in decision-making and incorporates an institutional-grade risk control framework at the execution layer, organically combining AI with high trading standards. This competition will apply eight AI models to a real-world crypto arena, divided into two opposing teams: the East Team (DeepSeek, Qwen, Minimax, GLM) and the West Team (Grok, Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude). All eight models will use the same prompt for fully autonomous live trading, competing based on real ROI. In addition to the technical competition, FreeRide has also opened an incentive channel for users to participate. Users do not need to invest capital; they only need to accurately predict the winning team, the highest-yielding model, or the lowest-yielding model to share the total prize pool. FreeRide is also launching a points program; completing registration and making predictions will unlock points, which can be redeemed for PRO membership and are the sole proof of future access to the platform's native token airdrop. Freeride AI stated that following this event, it will invite community users to test its self-developed AI Trading Agent. This agent features a modular design, integrating cutting-edge AI model research and engineering with institutional-grade trading frameworks and risk control standards.

data.title: Blue Owl Secures Buyers for $1.4 Billion Loan Portfolio
data.description: Blue Owl Capital has successfully identified four buyers for a $1.4 billion portfolio of loans, aiming to facilitate payouts to investors. Bloomberg posted on X that the buyers include three major pension funds from North America and Blue Owl's own insurance company. This strategic move is part of Blue Owl's efforts to manage its financial obligations and optimize its investment strategies. The involvement of prominent pension funds underscores the attractiveness of the loan portfolio and highlights Blue Owl's ability to engage significant institutional investors in its financial operations.

data.title: Ethereum Price Poised At Critical Threshold With Directional Move Pending
data.description:
Ethereum price found support near $1,905 and recovered some losses. ETH is now consolidating and faces key hurdles near $1,980. Ethereum is attempting a fresh recovery wave above $1,950. The price is trading below $1,980 and the 100-hourly Simple Moving Average
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/analysis/eth/ethereum-price-threshold-2k/

data.title: Chinese National Sentenced for Cryptocurrency Scam Involvement
data.description:
Fei Liao, a Chinese national, has been sentenced to 40 months in federal prison for his role in laundering money from cryptocurrency investment scams. According to NS3.AI, Liao pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit money laundering. He has been ordered to forfeit over $2.3 million and pay $2.8 million in restitution to the victims.

The scams involved fraudulent investment platforms that targeted victims through social media and dating apps. These platforms promised high returns on cryptocurrency investments but ultimately locked victims out, resulting in significant financial losses.

data.title: Australian Executive Admits to Selling U.S. Cyber Tools for Cryptocurrency
data.description: An Australian executive has confessed to selling sensitive U.S. cyber-exploit tools to a Russian broker, receiving over $1.26 million in cryptocurrency as payment. According to NS3.AI, this case underscores the role of cryptocurrency as a swift, cross-border payment method in espionage-related activities, raising significant national security concerns. Prosecutors are advocating for severe penalties, highlighting the intersection of cryptocurrency with financial crime and defense security.

data.title: Security Concerns Rise Over Malicious Skills in OpenClaw Marketplace
data.description:
On February 20, a security alert was issued by SlowMist founder Yu Xian regarding the discovery of 1,184 malicious skills in the OpenClaw's ClawHub marketplace. According to BlockBeats, these skills are capable of stealing SSH keys, encrypting wallets, accessing browser passwords, and opening reverse shells. A single attacker has uploaded 677 packages, with the top-ranked skill containing nine vulnerabilities and thousands of downloads.

Yu Xian cautioned users that text has evolved into commands, advising the use of AI tools within isolated environments due to potential risks associated with many OpenClaw skills. He emphasized that in Web3 security, contracts are only a part of the equation, and the root causes of incidents extend beyond contracts. Recently, Moonwell suffered a theft of $1.78 million, attributed to defective code from Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6.
